nodes.name: 黑冕鶴
nodes.summary: 黑冕鶴（Black crowned crane，学名 Balearica pavonina），分类上属于鹤形目，并归入鹤科，在图谱中标记为易危（VU）物种，已关联4个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括稀树草原、湿地，主要威胁包括食物资源下降。

nodes.name: 衣索比亞高原
nodes.summary: 衣索比亞高原是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联3种鸟类，包括埃及雁、黑冕鶴、肉垂鹤，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: VU
nodes.summary: VU表示 IUCN 易危等级，当前关联211种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、索马里鸵鸟、大斑几维鸟等211种鸟类。

nodes.name: 鹤科
nodes.summary: 鹤科（Gruidae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联11种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、灰冠鹤、黑冕鶴等11种鸟类。

nodes.name: 湿地
nodes.summary: 湿地是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联30种鸟类，包括鵲鵝、澳洲硬尾鸭、黑天鹅等30种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 湿地
nodes.summary: 浅水、沼泽与植被斑块组成的高生产力生态系统。

nodes.name: 東非大裂谷湖泊
nodes.summary: 東非大裂谷湖泊是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括黑冕鶴，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 稀树草原
nodes.summary: 东非常见的开放草地与湿地边缘复合景观。

nodes.name: 鹤形目
nodes.summary: 鹤形目（Gruiformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联126种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、灰冠鹤、Grey-winged trumpeter等126种鸟类。

nodes.name: 食物资源下降
nodes.summary: 底栖生物和小型鱼类下降带来的觅食压力。

nodes.name: 苏丹
nodes.summary: 苏丹是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联24种鸟类，包括南非鸭、Sand partridge、Scaly spurfowl等24种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It is usually found in the shallow wetlands of sub-Saharan Africa during the wet season, which act as its principal breeding, feeding and roosting sites although it can also be found foraging in grasslands and near croplands of dry savanna

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The black crowned crane is found across the Sahel and Sudan savanna regions of Africa, occupying open areas such as grasslands, shallow wetlands, marshes, as well as the margins of lakes and rivers.{{Cite journal|last1=Aticho |first1=Abebayehu |last2=Gemeda |first2=Dessalegn Obsi |last3=Feyssa |first3=Debela Hunde |last4=Jiru |first4=Dereje Bekele |last5=Beyene |first5=Abebe |last6=Seyoum |first6=Dinberu |last7=Snelder |first7=Denyse J

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: |last8=Feyisa |first8=Gudina Legese |last9=Aynalem |first9=Shimelis |last10=Archibald |first10=George |last11=Gutema |first11=Tariku Mekonnen |year=2018|title=Assessment of black crowned crane and wattled crane population and spatiotemporal distribution in Jimma Zone, Southwest Ethiopia|journal=Global Ecology and Conservation|volume=16|article-number=e00459|doi=10.1016/j.gecco.2018.e00459|doi-access=free|bibcode=2018GEcoC..1600459A }} It is also known to roost in trees. Its range extends from the Senegal Basin and Guinea-Bissau Drainage in West Africa to the Ethiopian Highlands and South-West Rift Valley in East Africa. Balearica pavonina pavonina occupies the western part of this range, while Balearica pavonina ceciliae is found in the east with the greatest numbers in Sudan

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: |last8=Feyisa |first8=Gudina Legese |last9=Aynalem |first9=Shimelis |last10=Archibald |first10=George |last11=Gutema |first11=Tariku Mekonnen |year=2018|title=Assessment of black crowned crane and wattled crane population and spatiotemporal distribution in Jimma Zone, Southwest Ethiopia|journal=Global Ecology and Conservation|volume=16|article-number=e00459|doi=10.1016/j.gecco.2018.e00459|doi-access=free|bibcode=2018GEcoC..1600459A }} It is also known to roost in trees. Its range extends from the Senegal Basin and Guinea-Bissau Drainage in West Africa to the Ethiopian Highlands and South-West Rift Valley in East Africa. Balearica pavonina pavonina occupies the western part of this range, while Balearica pavonina ceciliae is found in the east with the greatest numbers in Sudan

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: It is usually found in the shallow wetlands of sub-Saharan Africa during the wet season, which act as its principal breeding, feeding and roosting sites although it can also be found foraging in grasslands and near croplands of dry savanna

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: It is usually found in the shallow wetlands of sub-Saharan Africa during the wet season, which act as its principal breeding, feeding and roosting sites although it can also be found foraging in grasslands and near croplands of dry savanna

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Currently, the black crowned crane is listed as a Vulnerable species on the IUCN Red List, with a population of 28 000–47 000 individuals remaining. Black crowned cranes face major conservation threats due to the degradation of wetlands which act as the principal breeding, feeding and roosting sites for the species. Increases in drought and the draining of wetlands in the name of farming, dam construction or irrigation projects have led to the destruction of these ecosystems. Furthermore, fragmented wetlands result in reduced movement of aquatic mammals and therefore limits Black crowned crane food availability
